# Questions in Livestream 178

Live Stream #178: Raising Samuel For Fun and Prophet

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hCdQe9q4A5w?t=527" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">8:47</a> | Did he deny that Luke the beloved physician wrote the third gospel? | He says no; he believes Luke the physician wrote Luke, while the earlier question was about whether Luke was Gentile or Jewish. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hCdQe9q4A5w?t=605" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">10:05</a> | Why do some amateur historians write like manifestos? | He says bad method and muddled thinking often produce muddled, manifesto-like writing. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hCdQe9q4A5w?t=735" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">12:15</a> | Would Jethro's priesthood have come through a line reaching back to Melchizedek? | He says no direct genealogical chain is implied, since Melchizedek's priesthood is not inherited like the Aaronic one. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hCdQe9q4A5w?t=982" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">16:22</a> | How would he define preternatural as opposed to supernatural or paranormal? | He defines supernatural as beyond nature, paranormal as beside the normal, and preternatural as something that does not fit ordinary nature but is not necessarily miraculous. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hCdQe9q4A5w?t=1261" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">21:01</a> | Which science-fiction tropes or themes are overdone or could be explored more? | He especially criticizes overused character resurrection because it weakens the significance of death. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hCdQe9q4A5w?t=1665" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">27:45</a> | Is Kenneth Kitchen holding an old view by denying Egyptian decline from Ramesses II to Ramesses III? | He says yes, and points to more recent research arguing for decline in Egyptian holdings after Ramesses II. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hCdQe9q4A5w?t=2006" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">33:26</a> | If the fall of man is not core doctrine, could sin in the world come from something like an angelic fall instead? | He says evil and decay could be connected with an angelic fall, but sin is specifically human and tied to the human fall. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hCdQe9q4A5w?t=2207" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">36:47</a> | Did Esau's sold birthright include the special covenant relationship with God? | He treats the birthright question as significant but distinguishes family inheritance from the covenant relationship with God. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hCdQe9q4A5w?t=2800" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">46:40</a> | Does Genesis show that Melchizedek and Abraham had met before Genesis 14? | He says the text does not clearly show a previous meeting, though Melchizedek may have known of Abraham by reputation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hCdQe9q4A5w?t=3033" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">50:33</a> | Does he have a favorite theory about what happened to Alexander the Great's body? | He says there are many possibilities but no certain answer and no personal theory he strongly holds. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hCdQe9q4A5w?t=3210" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">53:30</a> | What does the Greek word related to Obadiah 1:14 and an extrabiblical papyrus example mean? | He says the Common Era Greek evidence is outside his forte and gives only limited help rather than a firm answer. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hCdQe9q4A5w?t=3386" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">56:26</a> | What is the difference between non-order and disorder? | He says non-order is an unorganized blank slate, while disorder is previously ordered reality turned into confusion. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hCdQe9q4A5w?t=3997" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:06:37</a> | Is it more likely there were several Abrahams, or one Abraham honorifically transposed as the last of his line? | He leans toward a line of Abraham with the last one honorifically representing the line, while allowing other possibilities. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hCdQe9q4A5w?t=4097" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:08:17</a> | What is an example of a prophet using a scribe? | He points to Jeremiah using Baruch as a scribe in Jeremiah 36. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hCdQe9q4A5w?t=4186" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:09:46</a> | Is Moses implying that Lot going with Abraham was a bad thing in Genesis 12:4? | He says yes, because Abraham was told to leave his relatives and Lot should have been left behind. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hCdQe9q4A5w?t=4685" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:18:05</a> | Does the idea of death as separation persist into New Testament times? | He says yes, citing Paul and broader ancient views of death as separation of body and soul. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hCdQe9q4A5w?t=4955" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:22:35</a> | Is it true that Jericho was destroyed well before the Israelites arrived? | He says no, citing Lorenzo Nigro's report that there was a Late Bronze II B city at Jericho. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hCdQe9q4A5w?t=5525" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:32:05</a> | Are there good books on Christotelic readings of the Old Testament? | He suggests John Walton's Lost World books as examples of doing this well, though not as a dedicated sourcebook. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hCdQe9q4A5w?t=5814" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:36:54</a> | When Saul spoke to Samuel, was he speaking directly to Samuel or through the medium? | He says Saul was speaking to Samuel, while Samuel's words came through the medium. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hCdQe9q4A5w?t=6064" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:41:04</a> | Why was the medium surprised to see Samuel? | He says the exact reason is unclear, but suggests summoning a prophet may have been unusual even for a medium. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hCdQe9q4A5w?t=6646" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:50:46</a> | Did men in ancient Egypt get paternity leave when their wives or daughters were menstruating? | He says he has never seen evidence for that claim and does not know where it came from. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hCdQe9q4A5w?t=7183" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:59:43</a> | Is a chariot's lack of flexibility the chariot's fault or the driver's fault? | He says both, because chariots are fast on roads but poor off-road vehicles. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hCdQe9q4A5w?t=7305" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:01:45</a> | Should people do baptisms in the Nile? | He says no, especially for anyone not raised there, because of parasites and disease risks. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hCdQe9q4A5w?t=7814" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:10:14</a> | Since John 1 is a midrash of Genesis 1, should the Word be reviewed in functional rather than material context? | He says John 1 is a transcendent midrash that moves from functional creation language toward Christ's immanence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hCdQe9q4A5w?t=8103" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:15:03</a> | Did he find the source arguing Isaiah 1 and 2 were both written by Isaiah at different times? | He says not yet, but he knows it is in a commentary and will look it up. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hCdQe9q4A5w?t=8151" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:15:51</a> | If he is from a musical family and makes music, why is he not good at music theory? | He says he was not taught an instrument as a child and had to learn music theory as an adult. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hCdQe9q4A5w?t=8288" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:18:08</a> | Has he created only one publicly available album? | Yes. He says the album is Apex Predator under the name Velocirabbit. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hCdQe9q4A5w?t=8480" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:21:20</a> | What does he use to compose music? | He uses MuseScore and recommends it as freely available composition software. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hCdQe9q4A5w?t=9015" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:30:15</a> | If cherubim were mythic to Israelites, why did cherub imagery not violate the second commandment? | He says cherubim were not worshiped as gods, but functioned as beings giving God glory and guarding sacred space. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hCdQe9q4A5w?t=9164" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:32:44</a> | Was Mark the first Gospel written, and what does that mean? | He says the common view is Markan priority, which would mean Matthew and Luke used a reliable source rather than undermining them. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hCdQe9q4A5w?t=9698" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:41:38</a> | Why does Proverbs 21:19 go after women specifically? | He says the proverb targets a contentious and vexing woman, not women as a whole, and the same problem can apply to men. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hCdQe9q4A5w?t=9900" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:45:00</a> | Can you be a Christian and still be spiritually useless for the kingdom? | He says yes in a minimal sense, but treats that as missing the point of discipleship. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hCdQe9q4A5w?t=10368" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:52:48</a> | What about the objection that Yahweh was originally a Canaanite storm-warrior god? | He says Israel's use of local deity language is better understood as divine accommodation, not a fatal objection. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hCdQe9q4A5w?t=12109" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:21:49</a> | Have any Egyptian hieroglyphs been found depicting the Israelites? | He says there are possible depictions in Egyptian iconography and the name Israel appears in hieroglyphs on the Merneptah Stele. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hCdQe9q4A5w?t=12200" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:23:20</a> | Does Job 5:1 support calling out to the saints? | He says no; the verse predates later saint theology and is not about invoking saints. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hCdQe9q4A5w?t=12476" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:27:56</a> | Does the Berlin Pedestal have the earlier possible name Israel? | He says no, warning not to use it as evidence because the proposed reading is badly translated. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hCdQe9q4A5w?t=12598" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:29:58</a> | In what way is God using the word "know" in Genesis 22:12? | He says the Hebrew verb has a broad semantic range and here concerns God's demonstrated knowledge of Abraham's fear of God. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hCdQe9q4A5w?t=13177" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:39:37</a> | Could Eliphaz in Job 4:18 be speaking of prophets when he says angels or messengers? | He reads the passage as referring to heavenly servants or angels rather than prophets. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hCdQe9q4A5w?t=13681" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:48:01</a> | Did ancient Near Eastern people have names for their religions like Christianity, Islam, or Hinduism? | He says generally no; named religions are a later categorization, while ancient people spoke more of local cults or gods. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hCdQe9q4A5w?t=14243" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:57:23</a> | What are his thoughts on Philipp Mainlander and pessimism? | He says he does not know enough about Mainlander or his pessimism to answer. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hCdQe9q4A5w?t=14354" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:59:14</a> | What books do you recommend for Merneptah and the Ramesside economy? | He points listeners toward Jack J. Johnson on commodity prices in the Ramesside period. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hCdQe9q4A5w?t=14460" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:01:00</a> | What are his thoughts on the historicity and reliability of Esther? | He says many criticisms of Esther are poor because they attack claims the book is not actually making. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hCdQe9q4A5w?t=14573" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:02:53</a> | Does the serpent in Genesis 3 relate more to the snake in the Epic of Gilgamesh? | He says no; Genesis has an angelic being, while the Gilgamesh snake is simply a snake. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hCdQe9q4A5w?t=15180" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:13:00</a> | Does the Bible teach that anxiety is sinful? | He treats involuntary anxiety carefully and reads Matthew 6 in the context of serving God rather than Mammon. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hCdQe9q4A5w?t=15698" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:21:38</a> | Why does 2 Chronicles 5:10 say only the tablets were in the ark while Hebrews 9 mentions three items? | He treats Hebrews as referring to the broader sacred furnishings associated with the ark rather than requiring all three items to still be inside it later. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hCdQe9q4A5w?t=15999" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:26:39</a> | Does the lack of Jesus in the Dead Sea Scrolls pose a problem for biblical theology? | He says no, because most Dead Sea Scrolls predate Jesus or come from a community where Jesus would not be expected to appear. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hCdQe9q4A5w?t=16396" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:33:16</a> | What do you say to Christians who divide over old-earth creationism? | He calls that divisive behavior over a secondary issue. |
